title: BlackSky 2025 Full Year Results and 2026 Guidance
text: 2025 Full-Year Results: Full year 2025 revenue was $106.6 million, a company record, and net loss was $70.3 million, with adjusted EBITDA of $0.9 million. Q4 2025 revenue was $35.2 million, up 16% year over year, with a net loss of $0.9 million versus $19.4 million a year earlier, and adjusted EBITDA of $8.8 million. BlackSky reported year-end backlog of $345 million, up 32%, and cash, restricted cash, and short-term investments totaling $125.6 million. Initial 2026 Guidance (February 26, 2026): Guidance for 2026 calls for revenue of $120–$145 million, adjusted EBITDA of $6–$18 million, and capital expenditures of $50–$60 million. Updated 2026 Guidance (May 7, 2026): More recently, BlackSky raised its guidance: Full-year projected range raised to $130 million to $150 million, up from prior $120 million to $145 million range, representing over 30% growth at midpoint. Annual guidance increased to $12 million to $24 million adjusted EBITDA, with a 13% margin at midpoint. The company revealed a total backlog, inclusive of contracts signed post-quarter, of approximately $380 million, with a projected $90 million to be recognized in the current fiscal year.
